---
title: "UAS Spasial Kelompok 9"
author: "Faiz Iqbal I'tishom"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

Import Library
```{r}
library(readxl)
library(spdep)
library(sf)
library(sp) 
library(mapview) 
library(tibble) 
library(lmtest) 
library(car) 
library(pscl) 
library(GWmodel) 
library(mgwrsar) 
library(ggplot2) 
library(spgwr)
library(dplyr) 
```

Subset Data
```{r}
data_provinsi <- read_excel("C:/Uner/Semester 4/Analisis Data Spasial/Kuis, Tugas, UAS/Data Spasial_Kelompok 9.xlsx")
data_provinsi
```

Statistika Deskriptif Dataset
```{r}
summary(data_provinsi)
```

Subset File SHP untuk Pemodelan Spasial yang akan dibuat (Menggunakan File SH)
```{r}
peta_indonesia <- st_read("C:/Uner/Semester 4/Analisis Data Spasial/UTS/gadm41_IDN_1.shp")
```

```{r}
sf::sf_use_s2(FALSE)
spatialreg::set.ZeroPolicyOption(TRUE) # This is good practice
spdep::set.ZeroPolicyOption(TRUE) 
```

```{r}
# Display initial data structures for verification
print("--- Initial Data Provinsi Structure ---")
print(head(data_provinsi))
print(names(data_provinsi))
print(unique(data_provinsi$PROVINSI)) # Check original province names
```


```{r}
print("\n--- Initial Peta Indonesia Structure ---")
print(head(peta_indonesia))
print(names(peta_indonesia))
print(unique(peta_indonesia$NAME_1)) # Check original province names in shapefile
```


```{r}
# 1.2 Create Aggregate Variables from Columns 1-60
# Based on your latest clarification, we'll sum ranges of columns for the variables.
# ***CRITICAL ASSUMPTION:***
# - Columns "1.0" to "12.0" contain monthly 'Jumlah Wisatawan Nusantara' data.
# - Columns "13.0" to "24.0" contain monthly 'Jumlah Penumpang Pesawat' data.
# - Columns "25.0" to "36.0" contain monthly 'Pengeluaran Industri' data.
# --- Adjust the column ranges (e.g., seq(1,12)) if your data structure differs. ---

data_provinsi$jumlah_wisatawan_nusantara <- rowSums(data_provinsi[, paste0(seq(1, 12), ".0")], na.rm = TRUE)
data_provinsi$jumlah_penumpang_pesawat <- rowSums(data_provinsi[, paste0(seq(13, 24), ".0")], na.rm = TRUE)
data_provinsi$pengeluaran_industri <- rowSums(data_provinsi[, paste0(seq(25, 36), ".0")], na.rm = TRUE)
```


```{r}
# Define the variable names to be used in models (consistent and easy to reference)
Y_VAR <- "jumlah_wisatawan_nusantara"
X_VAR1 <- "jumlah_penumpang_pesawat"
X_VAR2 <- "pengeluaran_industri"
```


```{r}
# Cleaning for data_provinsi$PROVINSI
data_provinsi$PROVINSI_Cleaned <- toupper(data_provinsi$PROVINSI)
data_provinsi$PROVINSI_Cleaned <- gsub("PROVINSI ", "", data_provinsi$PROVINSI_Cleaned)
data_provinsi$PROVINSI_Cleaned <- gsub("DAERAH KHUSUS IBUKOTA ", "", data_provinsi$PROVINSI_Cleaned)
data_provinsi$PROVINSI_Cleaned <- gsub("NANGGROE ACEH DARUSSALAM", "ACEH", data_provinsi$PROVINSI_Cleaned)
data_provinsi$PROVINSI_Cleaned <- gsub("DI YOGYAKARTA", "YOGYAKARTA", data_provinsi$PROVINSI_Cleaned)
data_provinsi$PROVINSI_Cleaned <- gsub("DKI JAKARTA", "JAKARTA", data_provinsi$PROVINSI_Cleaned) # Common variant
```


```{r}
# Cleaning for peta_indonesia$NAME_1
peta_indonesia$NAME_1_Cleaned <- toupper(peta_indonesia$NAME_1)
peta_indonesia$NAME_1_Cleaned <- gsub("JAKARTA RAYA", "JAKARTA", peta_indonesia$NAME_1_Cleaned) # Common variant
peta_indonesia$NAME_1_Cleaned <- gsub("BANGKA BELITUNG", "KEP. BANGKA BELITUNG", peta_indonesia$NAME_1_Cleaned)
peta_indonesia$NAME_1_Cleaned <- gsub("KEPULAUAN RIAU", "KEP. RIAU", peta_indonesia$NAME_1_Cleaned)
peta_indonesia$NAME_1_Cleaned <- gsub("DAERAH ISTIMEWA YOGYAKARTA", "YOGYAKARTA", peta_indonesia$NAME_1_Cleaned) # Another variant
```


```{r}
# Display cleaned names to manually check for any remaining discrepancies
print("\n--- Cleaned Province Names (Data Provinsi) ---")
print(sort(unique(data_provinsi$PROVINSI_Cleaned)))
print("\n--- Cleaned Province Names (Peta Indonesia) ---")
print(sort(unique(peta_indonesia$NAME_1_Cleaned)))
```

```{r}
spatial_data <- left_join(peta_indonesia,
                          data_provinsi %>% select(PROVINSI_Cleaned, !!sym(Y_VAR), !!sym(X_VAR1), !!sym(X_VAR2)),
                          by = c("NAME_1_Cleaned" = "PROVINSI_Cleaned"))
```


```{r}
# Check for NAs in the dependent variable after merge (indicates unmatched provinces)
print("\n--- Provinces in shapefile that did NOT find a match in data_provinsi ---")
print(spatial_data[is.na(spatial_data[[Y_VAR]]), "NAME_1_Cleaned"])
print(paste("Initial number of provinces in shapefile:", nrow(peta_indonesia)))
print(paste("Number of rows in merged spatial_data:", nrow(spatial_data)))
```


```{r}
# 1.5 Handle Missing Values and Ensure Valid Geometry
# Remove rows where any of the key variables are NA (important for modeling)

# Get the attribute data as a regular data.frame (without the geometry column)
spatial_data_attributes <- sf::st_drop_geometry(spatial_data)

# Filter the *original* spatial_data based on complete cases in the attributes
# This ensures you keep the geometry for the filtered rows
spatial_data_filtered <- spatial_data[complete.cases(spatial_data_attributes[, c(Y_VAR, X_VAR1, X_VAR2)]), ]

print(paste("\nNumber of provinces after filtering NAs for modeling:", nrow(spatial_data_filtered)))

# Pastikan data spasial valid setelah filtering (penting untuk operasi spasial)
spatial_data_filtered <- st_make_valid(spatial_data_filtered)

# 1.6 Convert to 'sp' object for compatibility with some spatial packages
# Banyak fungsi GWR-related di 'spgwr' dan 'GWmodel' masih utamanya menggunakan objek 'sp'.
spatial_data_sp_filtered <- as(spatial_data_filtered, "Spatial")
```

2. EDA
```{r}
# --- 2. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) ---
print("\n--- 2. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) ---")

# 2.1 Summary Statistics
print(paste("\nSummary Statistics for", Y_VAR, ":"))
summary(spatial_data_filtered[[Y_VAR]])
print(paste("\nSummary Statistics for", X_VAR1, ":"))
summary(spatial_data_filtered[[X_VAR1]])
print(paste("\nSummary Statistics for", X_VAR2, ":"))
summary(spatial_data_filtered[[X_VAR2]])
```

```{r}
# 2.2 Histograms to check distributions
# Note: aes_string is used because variable names are in string format
ggplot(spatial_data_filtered, aes_string(x = Y_VAR)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "steelblue", color = "black", bins = 30) +
  ggtitle(paste("Distribution of", Y_VAR)) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
ggplot(spatial_data_filtered, aes_string(x = X_VAR1)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "darkgreen", color = "black", bins = 30) +
  ggtitle(paste("Distribution of", X_VAR1)) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
ggplot(spatial_data_filtered, aes_string(x = X_VAR2)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "orange", color = "black", bins = 30) +
  ggtitle(paste("Distribution of", X_VAR2)) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# 2.3 Scatter Plots to visualize relationships
ggplot(spatial_data_filtered, aes_string(x = X_VAR1, y = Y_VAR)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "blue") +
  ggtitle(paste(Y_VAR, "vs.", X_VAR1)) +
  labs(x = X_VAR1, y = Y_VAR) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
ggplot(spatial_data_filtered, aes_string(x = X_VAR2, y = Y_VAR)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
  ggtitle(paste(Y_VAR, "vs.", X_VAR2)) +
  labs(x = X_VAR2, y = Y_VAR) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# --- 3. Thematic Mapping to Identify Spatial Patterns ---
print("\n--- 3. Thematic Mapping ---")

# Interactive maps with mapview
#mapview(spatial_data_filtered, zcol = Y_VAR,
        #layer.name = paste("Jumlah Wisatawan Nusantara"))
```

```{r}
#mapview(spatial_data_filtered, zcol = X_VAR1,
        #layer.name = paste("Jumlah Penumpang Pesawat"))
```


```{r}
# Static maps with ggplot2
ggplot(spatial_data_filtered) +
  geom_sf(aes_string(fill = Y_VAR)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma", direction = -1, name = "Value") +
  labs(title = paste("Spatial Distribution of", Y_VAR, "per Province")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```


```{r}
ggplot(spatial_data_filtered) +
  geom_sf(aes_string(fill = X_VAR1)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", direction = -1, name = "Value") +
  labs(title = paste("Spatial Distribution of", X_VAR1, "per Province")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```


```{r}
ggplot(spatial_data_filtered) +
  geom_sf(aes_string(fill = X_VAR2)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "cividis", direction = -1, name = "Value") +
  labs(title = paste("Spatial Distribution of", X_VAR2, "per Province")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

```{r}
# --- 4. Spatial Autocorrelation Analysis (Moran's I) ---
# To identify if there are clusters or spatial patterns (Objective 1)
print("\n--- 4. Spatial Autocorrelation Analysis (Moran's I) ---")

# 4.1 Create a Spatial Weights Matrix (Queen contiguity is common for polygons)
# Defines who is a "neighbor" to whom.
lw <- poly2nb(spatial_data_sp_filtered, queen = TRUE)
r_lw <- nb2listw(lw, style = "W", zero.policy = TRUE) # Row-standardized weights
```


```{r}
# 4.2 Global Moran's I Test
# Tests for overall spatial autocorrelation (clustering or dispersion)
moran_y_var <- moran.test(spatial_data_sp_filtered[[Y_VAR]], r_lw, zero.policy = TRUE)
print(paste("\nGlobal Moran's I for", Y_VAR, ":"))
print(moran_y_var)
```


```{r}
moran_x1_var <- moran.test(spatial_data_sp_filtered[[X_VAR1]], r_lw, zero.policy = TRUE)
print(paste("\nGlobal Moran's I for", X_VAR1, ":"))
print(moran_x1_var)
```


```{r}
moran_x2_var <- moran.test(spatial_data_sp_filtered[[X_VAR2]], r_lw, zero.policy = TRUE)
print(paste("\nGlobal Moran's I for", X_VAR2, ":"))
print(moran_x2_var)
```

```{r}
# 4.3 Local Moran's I (LISA) for Cluster Identification
# Helps identify specific high-high, low-low, high-low, low-high clusters.
lisa_y_var <- localmoran(spatial_data_sp_filtered[[Y_VAR]], r_lw, zero.policy = TRUE)
# Add LISA results to the spatial data for mapping
spatial_data_filtered$lisa_i_y <- lisa_y_var[,1] # Local Moran's I statistic
spatial_data_filtered$lisa_p_y <- lisa_y_var[,5] # P-value for local Moran's I
```


```{r}
# Classify LISA results for a thematic map (simplified for significance at p < 0.05)
spatial_data_filtered$lisa_cluster_y <- "Not Significant"
spatial_data_filtered$lisa_cluster_y[spatial_data_filtered$lisa_p_y < 0.05 & spatial_data_filtered$lisa_i_y > 0] <- "High-High" # Hotspot
spatial_data_filtered$lisa_cluster_y[spatial_data_filtered$lisa_p_y < 0.05 & spatial_data_filtered$lisa_i_y < 0] <- "Low-Low"   # Coldspot
# You can add High-Low and Low-High outliers if needed for more detailed analysis:
# spatial_data_filtered$lisa_cluster_y[spatial_data_filtered$lisa_p_y < 0.05 & spatial_data_filtered$lisa_i_y < 0 & spatial_data_filtered[[Y_VAR]] > mean(spatial_data_filtered[[Y_VAR]], na.rm = TRUE)] <- "High-Low Outlier"
# spatial_data_filtered$lisa_cluster_y[spatial_data_filtered$lisa_p_y < 0.05 & spatial_data_filtered$lisa_i_y > 0 & spatial_data_filtered[[Y_VAR]] < mean(spatial_data_filtered[[Y_VAR]], na.rm = TRUE)] <- "Low-High Outlier"
```


```{r}
# Map LISA clusters
#mapview(spatial_data_filtered, zcol = "lisa_cluster_y",
        #layer.name = paste("LISA Clusters for", Y_VAR),
        #na.color = "grey") # Grey for not significant/missing data
```


5. Spatial Regression Models
```{r}
print("\n--- 5. Spatial Regression Models ---")

# Get coordinates of polygon centroids for GWR models
coords <- st_coordinates(st_centroid(spatial_data_filtered))
colnames(coords) <- c("X", "Y") # Rename columns for clarity
```

```{r}
# Construct the regression formula dynamically
model_formula <- as.formula(paste(Y_VAR, "~", X_VAR1, "+", X_VAR2))
```


```{r}
# 5.1 Global OLS Regression (Baseline for comparison)
print("\n--- 5.1 Global OLS Regression Results ---")
ols_model <- lm(model_formula, data = spatial_data_filtered)
summary(ols_model)
# Check for spatial autocorrelation in OLS residuals
print("\nMoran's I test on OLS residuals:")
lm.morantest(ols_model, r_lw, zero.policy = TRUE)
# If Moran's I on residuals is significant, a spatial model is more appropriate.
```

```{r}
# 5.2 Geographically Weighted Regression (GWR)
# Suitable for continuous dependent variables.
print("\n--- 5.2 Geographically Weighted Regression (GWR) ---")

# Determine optimal bandwidth using AICc criterion (can be computationally intensive)
# 'adapt = TRUE' means bandwidth is defined by a number of nearest neighbors, not fixed distance.
gwr_bandwidth_aic <- gwr.sel(model_formula,
                             data = spatial_data_sp_filtered,
                             coords = coords,
                             adapt = TRUE,
                             gweight = gwr.Gauss, # Gaussian kernel
                             method = "AICc")
print(paste("Optimal GWR bandwidth (adaptive, AICc):", round(gwr_bandwidth_aic, 2), "neighbors"))
```

```{r}
# Run GWR with the optimal bandwidth
gwr_model <- gwr(model_formula,
                 data = spatial_data_sp_filtered,
                 coords = coords,
                 adapt = gwr_bandwidth_aic,
                 gweight = gwr.Gauss,
                 se.fit = TRUE, # Calculate standard errors for coefficients
                 hatmatrix = TRUE) # Needed for local R-squared and diagnostics

# Print GWR results summary (includes R-squared, bandwidth, and some diagnostics)
print(gwr_model)
```

```{r}
# Extract and map the local coefficients and local R-squared
gwr_model_results <- as.data.frame(gwr_model$SDF)
spatial_data_filtered$gwr_intercept <- gwr_model_results$X.Intercept.
spatial_data_filtered$gwr_coef_x1 <- gwr_model_results[[X_VAR1]]
spatial_data_filtered$gwr_coef_x2 <- gwr_model_results[[X_VAR2]]
spatial_data_filtered$gwr_local_r2 <- gwr_model_results$local.R2
```

```{r}

print("\n--- Summary of Initial GWR (Adaptive Gaussian) ---")
print(gwr_model)

# Untuk membandingkan model, kita akan melihat nilai AICc dari setiap model.
# Semakin rendah nilai AICc, semakin baik modelnya.
model_comparison <- data.frame(
  Model = "GWR (Adaptive Gaussian)",
  Bandwidth = gwr_bandwidth_aic,
  AICc = gwr_model$results$AICc
)
```

```{r}
# --- 5.3.1 GWR dengan Fixed Gaussian Kernel ---
print("\n--- 5.3.1 GWR with Fixed Gaussian Kernel ---")

# Temukan bandwidth optimal untuk kernel Gaussian tetap (fixed)
bw_fixed_gauss <- gwr.sel(model_formula,
                          data = spatial_data_sp_filtered,
                          coords = coords,
                          adapt = FALSE, # adapt=FALSE untuk bandwidth tetap (fixed)
                          gweight = gwr.Gauss)

print(paste("Optimal GWR bandwidth (Fixed, Gaussian):", round(bw_fixed_gauss, 4)))

# Jalankan model GWR dengan bandwidth tetap (fixed)
gwr_model_fixed_gauss <- gwr(model_formula,
                             data = spatial_data_sp_filtered,
                             coords = coords,
                             bandwidth = bw_fixed_gauss, # Menggunakan bandwidth fixed
                             gweight = gwr.Gauss,
                             hatmatrix = TRUE)

print(gwr_model_fixed_gauss)

# Tambahkan ke tabel perbandingan
model_comparison <- rbind(model_comparison, 
                          data.frame(Model = "GWR (Fixed Gaussian)", 
                                     Bandwidth = bw_fixed_gauss, 
                                     AICc = gwr_model_fixed_gauss$results$AICc))
```


```{r}
# --- 5.3.2 GWR dengan Adaptive Bisquare Kernel ---
print("\n--- 5.3.2 GWR with Adaptive Bisquare Kernel ---")

# Temukan bandwidth optimal untuk kernel Bisquare adaptif
bw_adapt_bisq <- gwr.sel(model_formula,
                         data = spatial_data_sp_filtered,
                         coords = coords,
                         adapt = TRUE,
                         gweight = gwr.bisquare) # Menggunakan kernel Bisquare

print(paste("Optimal GWR bandwidth (Adaptive, Bisquare):", round(bw_adapt_bisq, 2), "neighbors"))

# Jalankan model GWR
gwr_model_adapt_bisq <- gwr(model_formula,
                            data = spatial_data_sp_filtered,
                            coords = coords,
                            adapt = bw_adapt_bisq,
                            gweight = gwr.bisquare,
                            hatmatrix = TRUE)

print(gwr_model_adapt_bisq)

# Tambahkan ke tabel perbandingan
model_comparison <- rbind(model_comparison, 
                          data.frame(Model = "GWR (Adaptive Bisquare)", 
                                     Bandwidth = bw_adapt_bisq, 
                                     AICc = gwr_model_adapt_bisq$results$AICc))
```


```{r}
# --- 5.3.3 GWR dengan Fixed Bisquare Kernel ---
print("\n--- 5.3.3 GWR with Fixed Bisquare Kernel ---")

# Temukan bandwidth optimal untuk kernel Bisquare tetap
bw_fixed_bisq <- gwr.sel(model_formula,
                         data = spatial_data_sp_filtered,
                         coords = coords,
                         adapt = FALSE,
                         gweight = gwr.bisquare)

print(paste("Optimal GWR bandwidth (Fixed, Bisquare):", round(bw_fixed_bisq, 4)))

# Jalankan model GWR
gwr_model_fixed_bisq <- gwr(model_formula,
                            data = spatial_data_sp_filtered,
                            coords = coords,
                            bandwidth = bw_fixed_bisq,
                            gweight = gwr.bisquare,
                            hatmatrix = TRUE)

print(gwr_model_fixed_bisq)

# Tambahkan ke tabel perbandingan
model_comparison <- rbind(model_comparison, 
                          data.frame(Model = "GWR (Fixed Bisquare)", 
                                     Bandwidth = bw_fixed_bisq, 
                                     AICc = gwr_model_fixed_bisq$results$AICc))
```


```{r}
# --- 5.4 Perbandingan Model GWR ---
print(model_comparison[order(model_comparison$AICc), ])
```


```{r}
# --- 6.1 Membuat Variabel Biner untuk GWLR (Tidak ada perubahan) ---
print("\n--- 6. Creating Binary Variable for GWLR ---")
median_y <- median(spatial_data_filtered[[Y_VAR]], na.rm = TRUE)
Y_VAR_BINER <- "wisatawan_diatas_median"
spatial_data_filtered[[Y_VAR_BINER]] <- ifelse(spatial_data_filtered[[Y_VAR]] > median_y, 1, 0)
spatial_data_sp_filtered[[Y_VAR_BINER]] <- spatial_data_filtered[[Y_VAR_BINER]]
gwlr_formula <- as.formula(paste(Y_VAR_BINER, "~", X_VAR1, "+", X_VAR2))
print("GWLR Formula:")
print(gwlr_formula)
```


```{r}
# --- Menghitung Matriks Jarak (dMat) (Tidak ada perubahan) ---
print("\n--- Calculating Distance Matrix (dMat) ---")
dMat <- gw.dist(dp.locat = coords)
```


```{r}
# --- 6.2 Menjalankan Model GWLR dengan Berbagai Bandwidth (KODE DIPERBAIKI) ---

# --- 6.2.1 GWLR dengan Adaptive Gaussian Kernel ---
print("\n--- 6.2.1 GWLR with Adaptive Gaussian Kernel ---")
bw_gwlr_adapt_gauss <- bw.ggwr(gwlr_formula, 
                               data = spatial_data_sp_filtered, 
                               family = "binomial",
                               kernel = "gaussian", 
                               adaptive = TRUE,
                               dMat = dMat) 
print(paste("Optimal GWLR bandwidth (Adaptive, Gaussian):", bw_gwlr_adapt_gauss, "neighbors"))

# PERBAIKAN: Hapus argumen kernel, adaptive, dan dMat dari ggwr
gwlr_adapt_gauss <- ggwr(gwlr_formula, 
                         data = spatial_data_sp_filtered, 
                         family = "binomial", 
                         bandwidth = bw_gwlr_adapt_gauss)
print(gwlr_adapt_gauss)
```


```{r}
# --- 6.2.2 GWLR dengan Fixed Gaussian Kernel ---
print("\n--- 6.2.2 GWLR with Fixed Gaussian Kernel ---")
bw_gwlr_fixed_gauss <- bw.ggwr(gwlr_formula, 
                               data = spatial_data_sp_filtered, 
                               family = "binomial",
                               kernel = "gaussian", 
                               adaptive = FALSE,
                               dMat = dMat)
print(paste("Optimal GWLR bandwidth (Fixed, Gaussian):", bw_gwlr_fixed_gauss))

# PERBAIKAN: Hapus argumen kernel, adaptive, dan dMat dari ggwr
gwlr_fixed_gauss <- ggwr(gwlr_formula, 
                         data = spatial_data_sp_filtered, 
                         family = "binomial", 
                         bandwidth = bw_gwlr_fixed_gauss)
print(gwlr_fixed_gauss)
```


```{r}
# --- 6.2.3 GWLR dengan Adaptive Bisquare Kernel ---
print("\n--- 6.2.3 GWLR with Adaptive Bisquare Kernel ---")
bw_gwlr_adapt_bisq <- bw.ggwr(gwlr_formula, 
                              data = spatial_data_sp_filtered, 
                              family = "binomial",
                              kernel = "bisquare", 
                              adaptive = TRUE,
                              dMat = dMat)
print(paste("Optimal GWLR bandwidth (Adaptive, Bisquare):", bw_gwlr_adapt_bisq, "neighbors"))

# PERBAIKAN: Hapus argumen kernel, adaptive, dan dMat dari ggwr
gwlr_adapt_bisq <- ggwr(gwlr_formula, 
                        data = spatial_data_sp_filtered, 
                        family = "binomial", 
                        bandwidth = bw_gwlr_adapt_bisq)
print(gwlr_adapt_bisq)
```


```{r}
# --- 6.2.4 GWLR dengan Fixed Bisquare Kernel ---
print("\n--- 6.2.4 GWLR with Fixed Bisquare Kernel ---")
bw_gwlr_fixed_bisq <- bw.ggwr(gwlr_formula, 
                              data = spatial_data_sp_filtered, 
                              family = "binomial",
                              kernel = "bisquare", 
                              adaptive = FALSE,
                              dMat = dMat)
print(paste("Optimal GWLR bandwidth (Fixed, Bisquare):", bw_gwlr_fixed_bisq))

# PERBAIKAN: Hapus argumen kernel, adaptive, dan dMat dari ggwr
gwlr_fixed_bisq <- ggwr(gwlr_formula, 
                        data = spatial_data_sp_filtered, 
                        family = "binomial", 
                        bandwidth = bw_gwlr_fixed_bisq)
print(gwlr_fixed_bisq)
```


```{r}
```


```{r}
```


```{r}
```


```{r}
```


```{r}
```


```{r}
```


```{r}
```


```{r}
```


```{r}
```


```{r}
```


```{r}
```


```{r}
```
